criteria.label: Open a PR with a thorough description (What / Why / How / Test plan) and green CI before requesting review.
criteria.example: Title: 'Add HNSW reranker for top-k retrieval'. Body includes eval delta and a test-plan checklist.

criteria.label: Keep PRs under ~400 lines and split bigger changes into a sequential stack.
criteria.example: Refactor → call-site updates → delete-dead-code as three sequential PRs instead of one 1200-line monster.

criteria.label: Give review comments with explicit intent markers (blocking / suggestion / nit).
criteria.example: `[nit] consider renaming x to chunk`. `[blocking] empty-list case isn't handled`.

criteria.label: Configure branch protection on important branches (require PR, ≥1 review, green CI, no force-push).
criteria.example: GitHub: Settings → Branches → Add rule for `main` with required status checks for `ci/tests` and `ci/lint`.

criteria.label: Use CODEOWNERS to auto-route reviews to the right team for each path.
Treat code review as a collaboration tool: ask questions, acknowledge praise, decline reviews you can't do well.
criteria.example: 'Don't have prompt context for this — reassigning to @alice' is a better outcome than rubber-stamping LGTM.
